author: @ryanlebeck259
text:
How long after the lamp is removed does the output drop?

I noticed on the video that the charge held for some time without falling after the lamp was removed.

Perhaps extended charge properties is another area to study?

All in all, well done you.

author: @fabrizioricciarelli
text:
Dear Robert, as usual you shocked me, in the positive meaning  of course ;-)
Ok, it seems that everytime you enter in contact with some material in its nanoparticle shape, you discover something interesting...isn't it? 
Well, you're a fantastic chemist so, why you don't ALWAYS make use of the nanoparticles version of every material when you prepare your mixtures, e.g. when you add some dopants?
Think about using nano-manganese sulphate, nano-zinc oxide, nano-formic acid, nano-thiourea...
Work on it mate, you'll have a lot of satisfactions!

author: @Fire72996
text:
And can i ask what does Dopants mean?
is it just a metal that act like paint?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: tiny additives - little bits of something you add to the main body of the material to see what effect it will have - so adding 0.5% of graphene to plastic makes it 35% stronger - the graphene is the dopant and the plastic is the body - the effect is an improvement in strength - usually they are added to active materials to change the activity

author: @billyc4111
text:
Hi robert
Basic i know but can the magan from old batteries be used for this ? it is really interesting and I  can see why you are excited by it. Also could you please steer me to how to make the zinc compound you used or have i missed some vids mate. Kind regards bill

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
hiya mate the exact details on how to make it are in this video The Birth Of A New Type Of Solar Cell ?
You need a soluble salt i used manganese sulphate the stuff in batteries is manganese oxide which is insoluble - nurdrage has a good video on how to convert one to the other

author: @thagrit
text: Does this make you regret chucking out your mistakes in the past? Or have you always investigated the results from mistaken steps?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: to be honest mate - I usually have a look at one or two things but often I will be working on something and realise something i did before would be good. But I keep a video log of the work i do - a kind of day in the life thing so I can review stuff. I did think of posting one but the quality is pretty poor and i do ramble a lot lol

author: @ehochimalpi
text:
He Robert,  I am gerhard rogen, I send you a link by google+ for my type of solarcell,

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: hiya Gerhard - I watched them all mate - good stuff - I got 0,3 volts - at the moment this isn;t anything to get too excited about - it is in the realms of interesting just now lol - lots of work to do with it - but one idea frequently sparks another and as you can see the magnetic alignment is very interesting and a lot of peole have been and are now going to apply that to different things
